sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: WuXi AppTec highlights the evolving landscape of small molecule drug discovery, emphasizing the increasing structural complexity of modern therapeutics such as targeted protein degraders and covalent agents. The contract research, development, and manufacturing organization notes that classical workflows are insufficient for induced-proximity therapeutics, which require specialized handling of ternary complex formation and linker topology. Dr. Dave Madge, VP of Discovery Services at WuXi AppTec, states that the field is shifting toward a deliberate discovery paradigm where screening, structural biology, and systems-level analysis make processes more predictable. To address translational success rates, the company advocates for integrated infrastructure connecting chemical synthesis, computational modeling, and manufacturing under one global quality system. This approach allows process decisions made early in discovery to carry forward without variability, compressing timelines for complex molecules like protein degraders. The organization supports biotech and pharmaceutical innovators across 30+ countries, aiming to ensure reproducibility from laboratory to commercial scale. By aligning discovery, development, and manufacturing, WuXi AppTec facilitates the progression of these advanced modalities into the clinic, addressing challenges such as molecular weight, polarity, and conformational flexibility that traditional medicinal chemistry struggles to manage effectively.

points.text: WuXi AppTec — Targeted Protein Degraders — Disease-relevant proteins — Integrated CRDMO support for clinic entry
WuXi AppTec: Providing integrated end-to-end CRDMO services for complex small molecules including protein degraders.
Targeted protein degraders convert a transient binding event into catalytic removal of a target protein; challenges include ternary complex formation and linker topology.
